title: BlackBerry Turns Page on Restructuring, Posts Strong Fourth Quarter
text: BlackBerry turned the page on restructuring and posted strong fourth quarter results, signaling that the company's turnaround is gaining momentum. The Waterloo-based company delivered strong fourth-quarter results with revenue increasing 10% to $156 million and adjusted EBITDA surging 71% to $36.1 million. The company's adjusted earnings per share of $0.06 exceeded analyst expectations of $0.04. The QNX embedded systems unit achieved record quarterly revenue of $78.7 million, up 20% year-over-year. The Secure Communications division posted 8% revenue growth to $72.5 million with 55% EBITDA growth. Major contracts include expanded partnerships with Canadian federal agencies through 2033 and sales to the German government. The company's overall strategy focuses on digital sovereignty—governments worldwide increasingly prioritize maintaining critical communications on domestically-controlled infrastructure. This positioning aligns BlackBerry's capabilities with geopolitical demands for secure communications. The company expects to continue generating strong cash flow and delivering profitability in the coming quarters.
